prompt: Translate the following English sentence into French, Japanese, and Arabic, and add a brief note on any phrase that was tricky to render faithfully: "The silence between us said more than any argument could have."
response:
French
- Le silence entre nous en disait plus que n'aurait pu le faire n'importe quelle dispute.

Japanese
- 私たちの間の沈黙は、どんな言い争いよりも多くを物語っていた。

Arabic
- كان صمتنا أبلغَ من أيّ جِدالٍ.

Brief notes on tricky points
- "said" is figurative here (silence "communicated" more), so I avoided literal verbs for "say" and used idiomatic verbs: French "en disait", Japanese "物語っていた/語っていた" (told/uttered metaphorically), Arabic "أبلغَ" (was more eloquent).
- "Argument" can be rendered several ways: French "dispute" (quarrel) or "débat" (debate); Japanese "言い争い／口論／議論" have different tones (quarrel vs. discussion); Arabic "جدال" suits "argument/quarrel." I chose words that match the likely emotional sense of the original.
